Part B.key_evidence: Science and difference cutouts appear as noise with no clear point source at center, while the template shows a bright saturated/diffuse blob with diffraction features. magpsf=14.50 is extremely bright, sigmapsf large (0.19), fwhm=4.89 pix is broad. ndethist=1 out of ncovhist=956 visits indicates a one-off detection at a position heavily observed. PS1 neighbor is 11.9 arcsec away (sgscore1=0.81) with missing g/r mags — likely a bright saturated star nearby.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Most consistent with a bogus/artifact detection: the reference shows a very bright star causing saturation/scattered light, and the negative subtraction at mag ~14.5 with only 1/956 detection history strongly suggests a subtraction artifact from the bright neighbor rather than a genuine astrophysical transient.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An alternative would be a real stellar variable (sgscore1=0.81 suggests a star nearby), but the extreme brightness, lack of a clear central PSF in science, and one-off detection among hundreds of visits make this far less likely than an artifact.
